title: Wisdom
description: The skill for living that begins with the fear of the LORD. Biblical wisdom is not abstract philosophy but practical knowledge rooted in covenant relationship — knowing how to navigate life in alignment with the Creator's design.

stops.label: Fear of the LORD
stops.development: The programmatic statement of Israel's wisdom tradition: 'The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge.'
stops.what_changes: Wisdom is anchored in theology, not mere pragmatism. True knowledge begins with right relationship to God.

stops.label: Wisdom Personified at Creation
stops.development: Wisdom speaks as a person present at creation: 'Before the mountains were settled... I was there.' She was 'beside him, like a master workman.'
stops.what_changes: Wisdom is elevated from practical skill to cosmic principle. The created order itself embodies divine wisdom.

stops.label: Where Is Wisdom Found?
stops.development: A hymn to wisdom's inaccessibility. No human effort can discover it; 'God understands the way to it.' The conclusion: fear the LORD.
stops.what_changes: The limits of human wisdom are exposed. True wisdom is not achievable by human striving but received as divine gift.

stops.label: Vanity of Vanities
stops.development: Qoheleth declares all things 'hevel' — vapor, breath, meaningless under the sun. The wisdom tradition confronts its own limits.
stops.what_changes: Wisdom literature gains its necessary counterpoint. Conventional wisdom cannot explain all of life's inequities.

stops.label: The Whole Duty of Humanity
stops.development: After exhaustive investigation, the conclusion: 'Fear God and keep his commandments, for this is the whole duty of man.'
stops.what_changes: Skeptical wisdom circles back to faith. Even the most rigorous questioning ends at the same starting point as Proverbs 1:7.

stops.label: Greater Than Solomon
stops.development: Jesus declares that 'something greater than Solomon is here.' The queen of the South came for Solomon's wisdom; a greater wisdom is now present.
stops.what_changes: Wisdom is no longer merely a tradition or a body of teaching but a person. Christological wisdom supersedes sapiential wisdom.

stops.label: Foolishness of God
stops.development: The cross is 'foolishness to those who are perishing' but 'the power of God' to those being saved. God's wisdom inverts human categories.
stops.what_changes: The cross redefines wisdom entirely. What appears as ultimate folly — a crucified Messiah — is revealed as ultimate wisdom.

stops.label: All Treasures of Wisdom in Christ
stops.development: In Christ 'are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.' Proverbs 8's personified Wisdom finds its ultimate referent.
stops.what_changes: The entire wisdom tradition is christologically fulfilled. The search for wisdom ends in a person, not a principle.
